title: IR: Add !nofpclass metadata
author: arsenm
description:
This adds the analogous metadata to the nofpclass attribute
to assert values are not a certain set of floating-point classes.
This allows the same information to be expressed if a function
argument is passed indirectly. This matches the bitmask encoding
of nofpclass.

I also think this should be allowed for stores to symmetrically handle
sret, but leave that for later.

Alternatively we could add a more expressive !fprange metadata,
but that would be much more complex. It's useful to match the attribute,
and more annotations can always be added.

Fixes #133560

tests.test_body:
declare void @use.i32(i32)

declare void @use.p32(ptr)

declare void @use.f32(float)

define hidden void @callee(ptr %p1, ptr %p2, ptr %p3, ptr %p4, ptr %p5, ptr %p6, ptr %p7, ptr %p8, ptr %p9, ptr %p10, ptr %p11) {
  %v1 = load i32, ptr %p1, align 4, !range !0
  %v2 = load i32, ptr %p2, align 4, !range !0, !noundef !1
  %v3 = load ptr, ptr %p3, align 8, !nonnull !1
  %v4 = load ptr, ptr %p4, align 8, !nonnull !1, !noundef !1
  %v5 = load ptr, ptr %p5, align 8, !dereferenceable !2
  %v6 = load ptr, ptr %p6, align 8, !dereferenceable_or_null !2
  %v7 = load ptr, ptr %p7, align 8, !align !3
  %v8 = load ptr, ptr %p8, align 8, !align !3, !noundef !1
  %v9 = load ptr, ptr %p9, align 8, !noundef !1
  %v10 = load ptr, ptr %p10, align 8, !nontemporal !4
  %v11 = load float, ptr %p11, align 4, !nofpclass !5
  call void @use.i32(i32 %v1)
  call void @use.i32(i32 %v2)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v3)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v4)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v5)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v6)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v7)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v8)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v9)
  call void @use.p32(ptr %v10)
  call void @use.f32(float %v11)
  ret void
}

!0 = !{i32 0, i32 4}
!1 = !{}
!2 = !{i64 8}
!3 = !{i64 4}
!4 = !{i32 1}
!5 = !{i32 3}

define float @nofpclass(ptr %p, i1 %C) {
block1:
  br i1 %C, label %block2, label %block3

block2:                                           ; preds = %block1
  br label %block4

block3:                                           ; preds = %block1
  store float 0.000000e+00, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %block4

block4:                                           ; preds = %block3, %block2
  %PRE = load float, ptr %p, align 4, !nofpclass !0
  ret float %PRE
}

!0 = !{i32 3}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define float @nofpclass_md(i1 %b, ptr %y) {
entry:
  br i1 %b, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %entry
  %not.nan = load float, ptr %y, align 4, !nofpclass !0
  br label %return

if.end:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %not.inf = load float, ptr %y, align 4, !nofpclass !1
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %if.end, %if.then
  %retval.0 = phi float [ %not.nan, %if.then ], [ %not.inf, %if.end ]
  ret float %retval.0
}

!0 = !{i32 547}
!1 = !{i32 519}

%struct.S1 = type { i32, float }

define float @phi_load_nofpclass_metadata(ptr %s1, ptr %s2, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %tobool = icmp eq i32 %c, 0
  br i1 %tobool, label %if.end, label %if.then

if.then:                                          ; preds = %entry
  %i = getelementptr inbounds %struct.S1, ptr %s2, i64 0, i32 1
  %val = load float, ptr %i, align 4, !nofpclass !0
  br label %return

if.end:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %val2 = load float, ptr %s1, align 4, !nofpclass !1
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %if.end, %if.then
  %retval = phi float [ %val, %if.then ], [ %val2, %if.end ]
  ret float %retval
}

!0 = !{i32 3}
!1 = !{i32 519}

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body: I guess we can leverage this metadata to infer nnan/ninf for phi nodes in SROA.
